beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: Mentor
beats.text: At thirty-four, with nine months of savings and a family on the horizon, your runway is actually closer to four.

beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: Sarcastic buddy
beats.text: Mentor is spot on; nine months of runway is a fantasy when you're factoring in diapers, baby gear, and actual business expenses.

beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: 18-year-old you
beats.text: Mentor, nine months is a hard, verified fact today, and waiting for a baby that isn't even here yet makes our runway zero.

beats.label: Opening
beats.speaker: Scared parents
beats.text: Eighteen-year-old, a baby isn't "zero runway," it is a real, high-cost health insurance fact we cannot ignore while planning this transition.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: Mentor
beats.text: Eighteen-year-old, the baby isn't here, but the health insurance shift is instant. Losing employer coverage shrinks your nine months to five, today.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: Sarcastic buddy
beats.text: Mentor, you are giving him way too much credit; that five-month runway assumes he actually launches something instead of just reorganizing his Figma files.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: 18-year-old you
beats.text: Sarcastic buddy, you're right. Our real risk isn't the baby; it's us playing pretend founder for five months without shipping a damn thing.

beats.label: Rebuttal
beats.speaker: Scared parents
beats.text: Eighteen-year-old, shipping is great, but if you get sick or hurt while playing founder, that unprotected fall ruins your family's future instantly.

beats.label: Verdict
beats.speaker: Council chair
beats.text: The time to build is now, but you must transition with a safety net, not a blind leap of faith.

verdict.decision: Yes, start your business, but transition part-time first to preserve health insurance and capital.
verdict.conditions: You must secure independent health insurance and ship a paying beta before leaving your day job.
verdict.firstMove: Draft a budget detailing family health insurance costs and define your minimum viable product scope tonight.
verdict.flipRisk: A confirmed pregnancy or a drop in savings below six months before the first sale.

alignment.keyConcerns: Unrealistic runway calculations and immediate loss of employer-sponsored health insurance.
Wasting precious runway on perfectionism and fake work instead of shipping.
Allowing fear and endless planning to kill the dream of ownership.
Exposing a growing family to catastrophic financial and medical risks.
